title: rubus hirtus var. subaxillaris
intro: Rubus hirtus var. subaxillaris, also known as the Himalayan black raspberry, is a thorny shrub native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its dark purple fruit and medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. subaxillaris include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
